description: Upstream BMS (/SESOWABMSPD04) polled the wrong register for ~87 days. Raw hourly samples alternate between the real counter (~7k MWh band), a near-zero artifact (<100), and a stuck-high value (~805k). At daily V_LAST aggregation only ~5 of 87 corruption-window days landed in the real-register band. The stitched :d is produced by a 3-segment split with an interpolate patch across the corruption window: :d.A (slice valid_to=2025-07-15) + :d.patch (interpolate valid_from=2025-07-15 valid_to=2025-10-09, source=:d.raw) + :d.B (slice valid_from=2025-10-09). interpolate uses the last clean reading BEFORE valid_from (2025-07-14=7187.17) and the first reading AT/AFTER valid_to (2025-10-09=7671.14) as endpoints and linearly fills the 86-day gap at ~5.56 MWh/day — cumulative preserved exactly at both boundaries, no artifact values participate. Counter is monotone over the whole year; annual integral is best-effort linear. Prior approaches tried and rejected: (a) bracket on :d.raw kept only samples in the endpoint value-range but rolling_sum's concat-with-anchor-offset stitch sort of all source rows can't compose a bracket-overlay with a full-period raw source — produces artefacts at alternating in-band/out-of-band samples; (b) hourly ingestion (53 of 87 corruption-window days have ≥1 real-register sample in the hourly extract at reference/snowflake_meter_readings/B217.Å1_VM71.csv) would allow bracket to keep real samples but requires ingesting hourly data and changing the ontology's aggregate field. interpolate was chosen because it matches existing primitives and doesn't depend on how many samples survive inside the window; only the two outside-window endpoints matter.
Counter froze 2026-02-19 at value 12121.072. Ontology stitches raw segment (:d.raw, valid_to 2026-02-19) with a children-sum patch (:d.patch, sum of B217.Å1_VMM71:d.D) via rolling_sum.

Children-sum patch: recovers B216 consumption from its sole child B217.Å1_VMM71 after B216's counter froze. Assumes B217 captures all B216 downstream flow; the ~28 MWh Feb-2026 vs-Excel delta is the pre-freeze (Feb 1 → Feb 18) portion that Excel's STRUX register-diff absorbed as zero.
Counter frozen at 12737.1533 for the entire observed window; last real reading predates the 2025-01-01 ingest start. Patched from child B327.Å1_VMM71 via sum from 2026-02-07 onward.

Children-sum patch: B327.Å1_VMM71 (sole child) stands in for B308's frozen counter. Produces meter_net ≈ 0 (as children equal parent). Feb-2026 Excel shows −30.78 vs ontology −8.98 MWh; the ~22 MWh gap is pre-patch Feb-1 → Feb-7 that the ontology does not reconstruct.

Trunk ÅNGA counter frozen at 2227.06 from 2025-01-01 through late October 2025 (+0.57 MWh drift over 10 months; effectively dead register), then unfreezes and ticks normally from 2025-11-01 onward. B307.Å1_VMM71 is a pool meter whose hasSubMeter children (B216, B302, B304, B311, B334, B337, B339) report real consumption throughout the freeze window, so meter_net on B307.Å1_VMM71 reads strongly negative (≈ −1500 to −2600 MWh/month, Σ ≈ −22 GWh) across Jan–Oct 2025. Not patched: true pre-November trunk consumption is unknown; patching to zero would mask the magnitude of the gap, and interpolating 2227 → the 2025-11-27 value of 8834 would spread ~6.6 GWh backward across a window where Excel itself reads zero. The former ann-snv-anga-b307-vmm71-swap annotation mis-categorised the 2025-07-31 segment boundary as a device swap: the register was already frozen pre-swap and continues flat post-swap until November, so the split straddles a non-event. Split kept in place; rolling_sum stitching is harmless on continuous values.

Post-unfreeze ontology totals lag Excel by ~3 months. Ontology ticks Nov=7.5, Dec=9.7, Jan=2.0, Feb=9.3 GWh. Excel shows zero until Feb 2026, then Feb=7.8, Mar=10.9, Apr=0.26 GWh — same cumulative ~27 GWh but shifted ~3 months later. Source of the offset unknown: delayed Excel invoicing, wrong Snowflake timestamps, or a genuine mid-window meter commissioning that accounting dated differently from BMS. Needs site verification before any corrective action.
Device register swap on 2025-07-31. Handled by 2-segment split :d.A | :d.B stitched via rolling_sum.
Device swap on 2026-01-13 (last value 3019.23). Handled by 2-segment split :d.A | :d.B stitched via rolling_sum.

Counter went offline 2025-07-31 at 328545. Downstream of B325 Panna2; no children to patch from — consumption beyond this date is unrecoverable from B390 alone. Leaf meter relative to its intended topology branch.
Counter went offline 2025-07-25 at 133.0. Leaf meter; no children available for a sum-patch.
Counter went offline 2025-11-26 at near-zero value (0.00213...). Leaf meter; no children available for a sum-patch.

Excel row 23 (B307 pool formula) subtracts B330.Å1_VMM71, and row 46 (B337) subtracts the same meter — B330 is double-counted in the Excel totals. Ontology attaches B330 to B337 per decisions.md 2026-04-20; B307 therefore over-counts in topology by Δ(B330) each month (~0.9 MWh Jan-2026, ~27 MWh Feb-2026). Excel is immutable; this discrepancy is expected and permanent.

Direction conflict between flow_schema and naming extractor for the B311.Å1_VMM70 ↔ B311.Å1_VMM71 edge. Not yet resolved in decisions.md. Does not affect Jan/Feb 2026 totals because both meters are leaf-level relative to their building's accounting.

Bracket patch: parameter-free monotone value-range clip over :d.raw's readings. Endpoints (v_lo, v_hi) derive from the source's readings immediately outside the validity window — the last clean pre-corruption value and the first clean post-corruption value. Any reading whose value falls outside [v_lo, v_hi] is physically impossible for a monotone counter and is dropped.

Counter offline 2025-09-21 through 2026-01-13 (~116 days), full catchup flush on 2026-01-14 (+135.62 kWh). Total preserved: 135.75 kWh ÷ 116 days = 1.17 kWh/day, matching neighbor rates (~1.2/day). Patched via interpolate between clean endpoints 2025-09-20 (483.323) and 2026-01-14 (619.070).

Partial data loss: counter flat 2025-07-14 through 2025-07-23 (~10 days), resumed with only +2.7 kWh caught up across Jul 24-25. Pre/post neighbor rate ~1.85/day suggests ~22 kWh expected; ~19 kWh was lost (counter stopped counting, not just reporting). Not patched via interpolate — that would under-distribute. Magnitude is small (~0.05% of annual) so accepted as-is.
